findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 SSRF check omits BLOCKED_HOSTS, bypassing metadata-IP block
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped branch re-checks only is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast on the mapped address but never re-checks the mapped IPv4 against BLOCKED_HOSTS. 100.100.100.200 (Alibaba/Tailscale metadata) is blocked ONLY by the string check at line 77, which sees '::ffff:100.100.100.200' not the dotted form. Confirmed live: validate_arr_url('http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:7878') returns (True, '').
findings.fix_hint: also reject when str(mapped) in BLOCKED_HOSTS so ::ffff:100.100.100.200 and other mapped metadata IPs are caught
findings.why_it_matters: An attacker configuring an *arr URL of http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]:PORT reaches a cloud metadata service that BLOCKED_HOSTS was specifically added to prevent — the exact SSRF class D-10 aimed to strengthen.
source_window: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
findings.canonical_line_content: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: IPv4-mapped block decision keys on raw hostname, so expanded-hex mapped form also bypasses BLOCKED_HOSTS
findings.problem: Even after adding a raw-hostname BLOCKED_HOSTS patch, the fully-expanded hex form bypasses it: validate_arr_url('http://[0:0:0:0:0:ffff:6464:64c8]:7878') returns (True, '') — confirmed live. ipaddress parses it as the same ipv4_mapped 100.100.100.200 with all four predicates False. The block decision must normalize through the parsed mapped IPv4 (str(mapped)), not the raw hostname string.
findings.fix_hint: normalize the block decision through the parsed mapped IPv4 (str(mapped)) so both ::ffff: and expanded-hex encodings collapse to the same check
findings.why_it_matters: A hostname-string BLOCKED_HOSTS patch alone would still let the expanded-hex mapped form reach blocked metadata IPs.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: Non-dotted IP notations (decimal/hex/shorthand) evade IP blocking and resolve to loopback
findings.problem: ipaddress.ip_address() raises ValueError for '2130706433' (decimal), '0x7f000001' (hex), and '127.1' (shorthand), so they fall through the 'except ValueError: pass' branch as non-IP-literals — confirmed live all three return (True, ''). The OS resolver httpx uses resolves them to 127.0.0.1, defeating loopback/metadata blocking. This is pre-existing code (line 80, outside the diff hunk) but is the same SSRF-bypass class the diff addresses.
findings.fix_hint: normalize numeric/shorthand hosts before the ip_address check (e.g. socket.inet_aton / int-to-IP coercion), or resolve+re-check at request time
findings.why_it_matters: A settings-form submitter can reach loopback/metadata via http://2130706433 or http://0x7f000001, bypassing the SSRF filter entirely.
source_window: if addr.is_link_local or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_unspecified or addr.is_multicast:

findings.title: IPv4-mapped addresses bypass the explicit metadata blocklist
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped IPv6 check only rejects mapped addresses when the mapped IPv4 is link-local, loopback, unspecified, or multicast. It does not compare the mapped IPv4 string against BLOCKED_HOSTS, so 100.100.100.200 is rejected as an IPv4 literal but accepted as http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]. Verified validate_arr_url returns valid for that mapped URL.
findings.fix_hint: After mapped = addr.ipv4_mapped, reject if str(mapped) is in BLOCKED_HOSTS before allowing the URL. Add a regression test for http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200].
findings.why_it_matters: Preserves an SSRF path to a metadata service the code already intended to block.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: New ipv4_mapped predicate block is redundant with the base check and adds no protection
findings.problem: ipaddress reports is_loopback/is_link_local/is_unspecified/is_multicast as True on the base ::ffff: IPv6Address itself (confirmed: ::ffff:127.0.0.1 is_loopback=True, etc.), so line 82 already rejects every case lines 85-88 re-test. The mapped block as written never changes an outcome — it masks the real gap (bugs-001) that the check WOULD matter for.
findings.fix_hint: repurpose the mapped block to re-run the BLOCKED_HOSTS check on str(mapped) (the work the base check does NOT already do)
findings.why_it_matters: The redundancy is harmless at runtime but masks the metadata-string bypass; reviewers/tests see 'IPv4-mapped handled' and stop.
